rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/07/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Continued to allow e-fling to be processed, for judicial economy, and to allow time to respond to these notes. 1) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. 2) Petitioner needs to prove by admissible evidence that either the Probate Code 6124 presumption doesn't apply or the presumption needs to be rebutted.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FREDDIE PATRICK WILLIAMS JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: continued to 6/15/22. Counsel is directed by the Court to file an amended petiiton that removes any mention of distribution to the estate of freddie williams sr., as once he passed, the right to the property and income derived from that property was an asset of the estate of elizabeth williams, and the transfer of funds will be to that estate. the amended account must also explain why the rent entries after the PR was appointed as a fiduciary are not $1,700 per month. in addition, it should explain why those entries do not go past 3/24/19, since the property was occupied well past that date.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FREDDIE A. WILLIAMS SR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: continued to 6/15/22. Counsel is directed by the Court to file an amended petition that is captioned first and final report and account, petition for final distributions. that petition should include only the real property, no income or distributions. it must request distribution of the real property to the PR of the estate of elizabeth williams. (as noted by Renard Williams in 2014, Elizabeth had the legal right to own the house by community property law). statutory fees shall be based on the original I & A value. please note, the court is issuing an OSC re why it should not strike the additional two I & As filed in this case for listing assets not owned by the decedent on the date of death (2/4/19) and an increase in FMV for RP even though it had not been sold (5/7/19).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KATHERINE THOMAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if the administrator's second amended report should be approved and the proposed distribution granted, although no personal representative has been appointed yet for the decedent's recently deceased brother, Shawnee Thomas, and the calculation of the net distributable amount is $1.00 off in the second section "XXIII" on p. 11: the net distribution. This examiner has been unable to find anything in the records indicating how the administrator was related as a "nephew" of the decedent. None of the petitions he filed in either this case or in the Estate of Eddie Thomas (PES-20-303985 consolidated with PES-89-251760) indicates who is parent is/was, who was the decedent's sibling. [LRSF 14.18 C.3.] Who was the decedent's mother? Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 11th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSEPH FLOYD DEROUEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/21/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Notice of the petition hasn't been filed.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WOON FUNG CHAN REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions And Confirming Trust Asset
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/21/22. The third amended petition was filed on 4/29/22 (but not actually e-filed and available for viewing until 5/2/22), which was not at least 30 days before this hearing. An amending pleading requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] Furthermore, notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] The face of the Notice of Hearing specifically states that the person "has filed a petition," etc. (The petitioners are supposed to be the co-trustees, not "administrator".) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NANCY BOYKIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO DETERMINE SUCCESSION TO REAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The decedent died in 2017. In November 2021, the decedent's two children/heirs signed disclaimers of their interests in the decedent's co-op share with a right to occupy an apartment unit at 880 McAllister Street, San Francisco. All other issue of the decedent who would be the takers of disclaimed interests under Probate Code section 282(a) signed disclaimers between November 2021 and January 2022. The petitioner alleges that all the disclaimers were timely under section 279(b)(2) & (f), as the petitioner and his family members were not aware of the steps and process of transferring the decedent's share until the petitioner retained his attorney in April 2021. 2) The second amended petition identifies "Bethel Housing Corporation" as the issuer of the decedent's co-op share. However, documentation submitted by the petitioner in support of the decedent's ownership interest was a letter issued by "Freedom West Homes". PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 11th Amended General Order which took effect on 04/25/2022 and LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EVA C. TOLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing the estate is set for 5/16/2023, although the estate should be able to close much earlier after the creditors' claims period expires, as it does not appear that any estate tax will be due and especially in light the large assignments already made. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before 5/16/2023. Appearance will be required on 5/16/2023 if no petition has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SHIRLEY S.M. CHONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/21/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. ISSUES: 1) Petitioner William Chong must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 2) Petitioner William Chong must file proof of service of the summons. 3) Petitioner William Chong please file a complete copy of the 2016 trust and any and all amendments. 4) Petitioner Xue Jun Chong must file proof of service of the summons. 5) Petitioner Xue Jun Chong please give 15 days' notice to all beneficiaries named in the 1991 trust. (Probate Code 1202). 6) Petitioner Xue Jun Chong please file a complete copy of the 1991 trust and any and all amendments. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/6/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LEWIS AGUIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. a. Objector, Christina Marie Bryant, requests full bond and that Objector be included as heir-at-law prior to issuance of letters and appointment. b. It appears Evan D. Winet of Johnston, Kinney & Zulaica LLP represents both Christina Marie Bryant and American Research Bureau. Caption of Objections alleges Evan D. Winet is attorney for Christina Marie Bryant Objector and the signature page indicates Evan D. Winet is attorney for American Research Bureau, Objector. c. The Objector's "Agreement and Assignment" needs to be a stand alone filing and does not appear to comply with Probate Code 11604.5.
